mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and deteriorating corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing with p greater than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real short-term interest rates combined with accelerating retail sales indicate demand-pull inflationary pressure that forces monetary tightening, but the concurrent sales growth supports corporate revenues, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in greater than 55 percent of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with p greater than 0.10 in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth signals expanding manufacturing capacity and rising corporate output, which directly translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during economic recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery periods, with a hit rate below 50 percent and Rank IC consistently negative across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening economic activity and higher consumer spending capacity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows no correlation with 20-day forward returns with Rank IC below 0.01, or positive return frequency falls below 52 percent across all historical periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index crosses above 250, elevated inflation erodes real corporate margins and triggers aggressive central bank tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL levels above 250 consistently coincide with positive 60-day forward returns, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation with p greater than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts alongside falling unemployment indicate robust consumer confidence and strong credit conditions, jointly driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns in greater than 60 percent of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP exceeds 2.0 percent, confirming sustained economic expansion that supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 2.0 percent fails to predict positive 60-day returns during early-cycle phases, with a predictive accuracy below 50 percent and negative Rank IC.
